name: Puerto de Palomberas and Castillo de Argü
name_original: Puerto de Palomberas y Castillo de Argüeso – ruta circular
description: Hike the difficult 12.8-mile Puerto de Palomberas y Castillo de Argüeso circular route, featuring a historic castle and mountain pass.
region_intro: Hiking around Hermandad De Campoo De Suso offers diverse landscapes, characterized by a dramatic interplay of mountains and valleys in Cantabria, Spain. The region is shaped by rivers like the Híjar and Argoza, and features the traditional source of the Ebro River at Fontibre, surrounded by rich forests. High mountain plateaus, ancient oak and holly forests, and the towering Cordillera del Alto Campoo define the terrain. These natural features provide varied hiking opportunities across different elevations. Best hiking trails around Hermandad De Campoo De Suso The most popular hiking route is Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares from the collado de Fuente del Chivo, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of two prominent peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Puerto de Palomberas and Castillo de Argüeso, a difficult 12.8 miles (20.6 km) path. This longer trail combines mountain passes with historical landmarks. Local hikers also love the Tres Mares Peak – Tres Mares loop from Estación de Esquí y Montaña Alto Campoo, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail leading through high mountain terrain, often completed in about 56 minutes. Hiking around Hermandad De Campoo De Suso is defined by mountain peaks, river valleys, and ancient forests, with options for easy walks and more challenging ascents. The network offers routes suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Hermandad De Campoo De Suso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: Hermandad De Campoo De Suso offers over 25 hiking routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy walks, moderate trails, and some more challenging ascents through its diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: The region is characterized by a dramatic interplay of mountains and valleys, shaped by rivers like the Híjar and Argoza. You'll encounter high mountain plateaus, ancient oak and holly forests, and the towering Cordillera del Alto Campoo. Trails range from gentle paths through forests to more rugged ascents to mountain peaks.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001338682/puerto-de-palomberas-and-castillo-de-argue" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Palomberas and Castillo de Argüeso&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 12.8-mile (20.6 km) path. This trail combines mountain passes with historical landmarks and significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Hikers can explore a wealth of natural features and landmarks. The traditional source of the Ebro River at Fontibre is a serene spot surrounded by poplars, oaks, and beeches. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views, or discover the ancient 'El Abuelo' walnut tree in Hoz de Abiada. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt; also offers amazing landscapes.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find trails that lead to beautiful water features. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall loop from Río Hijar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4-mile (6.4 km) route that allows you to experience the region's cascades.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: The best time for hiking is generally during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn, when the high mountain areas are free of snow and the weather is most pleasant. While the Cordillera del Alto Campoo is known for skiing in winter, its summer landscape transforms into vast pastureland, perfect for exploration.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. The region's diverse network includes paths that are less strenuous, allowing families to enjoy the natural beauty together. Many trails through the forests and along river valleys are ideal for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: While specific dog-friendly regulations can vary by protected area, generally, dogs are welcome on most trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or park rules for any specific restrictions, particularly in nature reserves or areas with sensitive wildlife.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene river sources to panoramic mountain views, and the opportunity to explore ancient forests and historical sites. The variety of trails suitable for different abilities is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River – Refugio de La Guariza loop from Fontibre&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.5-mile (8.8 km) circular trail that allows you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Parking is generally available at common starting points for trails, especially near villages or popular attractions. For example, trails originating from Fontibre or near the Alto Campoo ski station typically have designated parking areas. It's recommended to check specific trailheads for detailed parking information before your hike.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible via hiking?
A: Absolutely. The majestic 13th-century medieval fortress, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001338682/puerto-de-palomberas-and-castillo-de-argue" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Argüeso&lt;/a&gt;, can be incorporated into hiking excursions. The region also boasts numerous Romanesque and Gothic churches, such as the Church of San Cipriano in La Población, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor exploration.
tips: The Palombera pass is a 1st category mountain pass located at the eastern end of the Sierra del Cordel. One of the most beautiful ports in Cantabria with a spectacular landscape.

On a hill, and guarding the road that linked the coast with Castilla through the Saja Valley, is the Castillo de San Vicente, in Argüeso, within the municipality of the Brotherhood of Campoo de Suso, in the South from Cantabria (Spain).
Built during the 13th, 14th and 15th centuries, it was the emblem and fortress of the dominion of the House of Mendoza in the highlands of Campoo.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular route usually takes around 6 hours and 8 minutes to complete. This estimate is for continuous hiking and does not include stops for rest, photos, or exploring the Castillo de Argüeso.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied. While generally accessible, sure-footedness is required, indicating that you may encounter uneven or rocky sections. The trail includes ascents and descents, typical of a mountain pass and castle approach.

faqs.question: Is the Puerto de Palomberas y Castillo de Argüeso – circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as a 'difficult' hike and requires 'very good fitness'. It spans over 20 kilometers with a significant elevation gain, making it unsuitable for beginners or those with limited hiking experience.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlights of this route are the historic Castillo de Argüeso, a Gothic-style medieval castle offering commanding views, and the Palombera Pass. The trail also offers diverse Cantabrian landscapes, potentially including green valleys, native forests, and riverside sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Puerto de Palomberas y Castillo de Argüeso circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, the Castillo de Argüeso is visible from the access road from Paracuelles, suggesting parking options might be available near the castle or in the village of Argüeso. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The available information does not explicitly state whether the trail is dog-friendly. However, as a general rule for challenging hikes in natural areas, if dogs are permitted, they should be kept on a leash to ensure their safety, protect local wildlife, and respect other hikers. Please check local regulations before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is generally during spring, summer, or early autumn when the weather is more stable and temperatures are milder. Winter hiking could present additional challenges due to snow or ice, especially at higher elevations like the Palombera Pass.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike the trail itself. However, if you plan to enter the Castillo de Argüeso, there may be an admission fee, as it functions as a cultural center.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route shares sections with other official trails. Approximately 36% of the route overlaps with the Transcantábrica trail, 18% with the Sendero Histórico: Cantabria, and a small portion (2%) with the [CIMA CA04] Palombera route.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is in a natural setting, the nearby village of Argüeso, home to the Castillo de Argüeso, is likely to offer some local amenities such as cafes, pubs, or accommodation options for hikers before or after their journey.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this difficult route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear with good ankle support, layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions, sun protection, a map or GPS device, and a first-aid kit. Sure-footedness is required, so sturdy boots are essential.
